title: extern shouldn't be used on member definitions
description: "extern" shouldn't be used on member definitionsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugunusedWhy is this an issue?More InfoData members and member functions cannot be defined as external, although entire objects can. When a member is declared asextern, the compiler simply ignores the keyword, making it both extraneous and confusing.Noncompliant code exampleclass C { void fun(); }; extern void C::fun() { // Noncompliant }Compliant solutionclass C { void fun(); }; void C::fun() { }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: extern shouldn't be used on member definitions - extern is ignored and confusing on member function definitions
